question: Does the v0.1 daughterboard interface support hot plugging?
context: The task forbids a hot-plug claim unless the electrical interface is explicitly designed and tested for it. The v0.1 interface has no inrush limiting, no supply sequencing, no connector with staggered mate-first ground pins, and no hot-plug test evidence.

alternatives.description: Declare hotPlugSupported=false. Daughterboards are inserted or removed only with the console switched off. Firmware displays/announces this in the classroom flow; documentation states it explicitly.
alternatives.tradeoffs: Pros: honest, zero additional design cost, matches PPTC-only protection. Cons: less convenient classroom workflow.

alternatives.description: Add staggered-ground connector, inrush limiter, ESD-rated transceivers, and a hot-plug test campaign.
alternatives.tradeoffs: Pros: better UX. Cons: cost and schedule; unjustified for v0.1 pilot volumes.

rationale: Without sequencing and inrush design plus test evidence, any hot-plug claim would be unsupported. SEC-INTERFACE-INTEGRITY-001 rejects hotPlugSupported=true whenever sequencing/inrush evidence is undefined, so the frozen product declares false. Revisiting requires the design-for-hot-plug alternative plus validation evidence.
tradeoffs: Classroom workflow requires power-off swaps; accepted for v0.1.

metadata.strappingPinNote: GPIO8/9 (I2C) strapping approvals are documented in pin-map.json and risks.md R-02; a daughterboard holding SCL low during reset can force download mode — bench obligation recorded.
